item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i see people have already discussed why this should / should not be a -J (i feel it should), so i will just leave my -1 for this being way too lolFoundation and move on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I… just don't get the humor here. I assume it's meant to be humorous anyway. Not a fan of "the foundation is compromised" articles, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
CaptainAmericaIUnderstoodThatReference.jpg

I wouldn't say it's woefully underpopulated, but the wiki can always use more bees. :V

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What I imagine went through the head of the person who wrote this before they uploaded it:
"What's this? A handsome horror wiki woefully underpopulated by bees? My anomaly full of bees should put a stop to that!"

Seriously though. With the addition of this anomaly, I now refuse to believe Dr. Bees does not live within the SCP universe. +1

item.title: Infohazard Article
item.content:encoded:
This whole SCP article is just one big infohazard. Clearly, the Land of the Tall Bees is tricking the researches there that they are not anomalous instances if they believe that the bees are tall.

I for one do not want to believe that the bees tall and see myself as a normal person SCP-3454 instance, just like Dr. Page SCP-3454-145.

With all that being said, this was a neat SCP article to read. +1

item.title: Tall Bees
item.content:encoded:
Still no tall bee pictures, and I don't believe. Guess I'm a 3454.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Someone needs to write an SCP about where the bees come from. Like not these specific bees, but just bees in general.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You are the hero we need. ;_;7

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think some of it stems from both the inherent weirdness of bees, as well as personal memories.

Incidentally, today I rescued a bee at work in the parking lot. It was lost and almost got run over.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As someone who's also written something about bees for this site, it's weird how much can be done with the concept. Something about bees just lends them to bizzaro fiction, whether it's horror or comedy.
Now everyone can see, that the bees, are tall. It is undeniable.

item.title: beeeeeeeeeeeeeeeee
item.content:encoded:
I have no idea what I just read.
Perhaps it simply went over my head.
Much like the bees.
(because they are tall)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Bees" is a funnier sounding word than "wasps".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The fact that the phrase Tall Bees rolls off the tongue so well is the hidden power of this SCP.

+Bees

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No, no, that's not what I meant-
Clearly it'd break their minds in a different way. This thing is like… it's like a claw hammer. And this claw hammer hits 100 different vases in the same place, with the same force. And every time, it makes the same cracks.
But then it comes across a different-shaped vase. Of course, it shatters, no problem. What is left of the fragments? Is it enough to construct something that makes sense (tall bees for everyone else)?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, it's not everyone; see also the claw hammer.

It would likely depend on who was in control. Do they rationalize that, oh, this person obviously could not understand the bees? Or do they simply find that they have another instance of SCP-3454 on their hands…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Disregarding the fact that EVERYONE, every single mind exposed to the Land, rationalizes the eldritch bee-ings as "tall bees" regardless of mindset, intelligence, rationality, etc…
What if we fed it a toddler, or a mentally retarded person, who doesn't understand the concept of "bees" or "tall" or both?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The best part for me is that it's unclear how the infection is spread. Is it just contact with someone else who is infected, or is it proximity to the portal? Something else entirely? Unfortunately we can't be certain, because the researchers were infected before they could find out realized it was far more important to properly convey that the bees are in fact quite tall.

Also, from a personal standpoint I find the concept of a mental hazard producing nonsensical things like "tall bees" a lot more compelling (and creepy) than "glory to Generic Otherworldly Being #89". With that you have a target, an explanation, a reason, or at least hint. This? Nothing. No idea what's really going on, if it's intentional or just an accident. The only thing we know is that it breaks brains and spreads that breakage, and we aren't even sure how (though apparently not via claw hammer).

+1.

EDIT: also….

You do not want tall, upset bees.

I can't really argue with that, so at least they aren't entirely crazy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This clearly isn't a -J, you've gotta read between the lines here.

The way I'm reading it, there are things on the other side of this portal that break people's minds. For some reason, these things can only be interpreted as "tall bees", whatever the fuck that even means, and exposure to them causes an all-consuming obsession with them. Now the whole site is compromised and is actively working to spread the infection.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You clever man. I see what you did there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: get out

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not a single redaction. Nobody can hide how tall those bees are.

I also really liked how those who don't exhibit anomalous properties are categorized as SCP-3454, and those affected by the anomaly are the ones categorizing.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Infohazardous bees, why does it sound so familiar to me? ( ͡° ͜ʖ ͡°)

It's not something bad though. In fact, I liked this skip. Mainly in a "OH WHAT THE…?!" way. I find it kinda creepy, mainly because of this wierd premise of tall bees (I actually don't like bees, even ordinary ones. I don't want to see those tall bees. Please no). So Im gonna upvote why not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 2. What makes you think that what they are interacting with on the other side is even physically measurable?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is actually a little unsettling despite the humorous aspects. The very long list of personnel involved in the last test really effectively conveyed that people's reasoning is compromised here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1) I absolutely took this story about a Foundation site being compromised seriously.

2) What makes you think anyone involved in this is well enough in their right mind to consider an option like that? Or, alternately, maybe they just haven't gotten around to it/logged the attempt yet.

Quirky does not equal -J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Two problems with this:

1 - It's on the mainlist and not an SCP-J. I could not bring myself to take the subject matter seriously, and, admit it, neither could you.

2 - After the tape measure test failed, they should have tried using a laser tape measure, then a military-grade handheld rangefinder. The maximum ranges for these top out at around 330 feet and 25km, respectively. I'd imagine a request for funding to build a LiDAR facility inside The Land of Tall Bees would then follow. This doesn't fix problem #1, but it would make the joke even funnier for an SCP-J.

Seriously, this is an SCP-J. Treat it like one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I blame Eddie Izzard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ohh, I get it. Well done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the bees…. are short

item.title: Re: Welp
item.content:encoded: I'm sorry. D:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has honestly always been my take on it as well.

item.title: Welp
item.content:encoded:
I remember being the person who asked for help with the tables. I therefore feel guilty about this.

And proud. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The article is too clever to ignore, I am very satisfied with how called-for the strike-throughs and footnotes are given the story. Also a nice sprinkle of a very ominous tone towards the end there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like to imagine that the large numbers of bee-related SCPs indicates that there is something deeply anomalous about bees in general in the foundation-verse, and the Foundation just hasn't put all the pieces together yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is this… an in universe example of the people tired by the Bee meme? I ain't even mad.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's a coffee shop in my neighborhood called Hive - their motto is 'the place to bee'. I may not be able to see that without thinking of this SCP.

Also, one early member of the Royal Society wrote about the proper model for natural philosophers (scientists). Not theoreticians, spinning webs in the air like spiders, nor artisans, working by routine like ants, but combining theory and practice to bring useful and beautiful things to the world, like bees, who fill their hives with honey and wax. No word on how tall they should bee, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All I'll say is there may definitely have been some influence from my favorite GoI format…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… this is like the bastard child of SCP format and Serpent's Hand tone…

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a Canadian, I can definitely say that they were.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To add onto this, I've heard that the stores were horribly managed and stocked, which may have contributed to a lack of tall bees.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Anomalous stuff involving bees has been done a lot now on this wiki, but this is all bizarre and humorous enough for me to dig it.

+Tall

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sounds like the bees weren't tall enough to attract people into buying from Target Canada.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As someone who has gotten lost in Target several times as a kid, I can confirm that Toa_Vine is tricking you and that Target bees are very very tall.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so goddamn weird. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They're popular outside of the wiki too, look at things like Bee Movie and the "bees are dying at an alarming rate" meme. I think it's got to bee human nature.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's a good question. Bees holds a place of hallowed legend on the wiki, but like… Did it start the trend, or was it reacting to something already present? In the wiki? In human nature?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so weirdly cute (aside from the claw hammer thing)! Definite +1. I guess the bees are too tall to come through the portal to our world, poor guys.

One thing I've never quite gotten: Why are bees always the insect of choice? Like if this was "tall wasps" I suspect I wouldn't be so fond of it, but because it's bees I love it. It's a strange power bees seem to have.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, so learning about Target Canada while trying to find an image for this article1 was very interesting.

It was a whole other branch of the Target corporation, and it lasted for maybe two years around 2014. Fuckin' all Targets in Canada are closed now, because they just didn't work out. People went to Walmart, or crossed the border for better prices because import hikes. Just, this whole major big-box chain that I am used to seeing in literally every city I go to tried to get a foothold north of the border and totally failed at it. Incredible.

Shared because all this trivia has to be used for something, so I might as well inflict it on you. Thanks for updoot. <3

Footnotes
1. I asked chat where a portal should be located and got 'a supermarket' and 'Canada'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've worked in a target before, and I assure you, our bees are not this tall.

+1

item.title: Have another fucking SCP about bees
item.content:encoded:
I was in chat one day and someone1 was asking for help with tables, but they spelled it 'talbes'. And then I was like DID SOMEBODY SAY TALL BEES? And then that kind of stuck with me for a few days and I decided to write a skip about it. Thanks to actual, for-real Hippo for some feedback that pushed me in a different direction to what you see now here before you.

Image from Wikipedia, by EquestriaGirlsFan2003 - Own work, CC BY-SA 4.0.2

Footnotes
1. I forget who, and I apologize, for numerous reasons.
2. I adore that username.
